rag_rules: Place input assumptions in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope; use component type annexes for output range guarantees
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the clause
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers from the AADL model for AGREE expressions
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatches
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Use named constants or visible relations for concrete bounds in assumptions and guarantees
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `assign` for implementation-level bindings
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantees in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
